properties.remark: EF0 TORNADO, 0.25 MILE DAMAGE PATH, ESTIMATED 75 MPH WIND SPEED. DAMAGE INCLUDES UPROOTED TREES, BROKEN CAR WINDOWS, AND BROKEN SIGNAGE. NO STRUCTURAL DAMAGE OR INJURIES.
PRELIMINARY REPORT OF A TORNADO NEAR MAXWELL.

FINAL REPORT FROM THE NWS DAMAGE SURVEY - AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN WILLIAMS. THE DAMAGE PATH WAS 3/4 MILE AND THE ESTIMATED WIND SPEED WAS 80 MPH. SIX HOMES WERE PARTIALLY DAMAGED IN ADDITION TO TWO CARS... AN OUT BUILDING... AND A RADIO TOWER.

AN NWS SURVEY DETERMINED A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN WILLIAMS. THERE WERE 6 HOMES PARTIALLY DAMAGED. THE DAMAGE PATH WAS ESTIMATED TO HAVE BEEN 1 MILE LONG. A PRELIMINARY ESIMATE HAS THE TORNADO AS AN EF0.
AT AROUND 352 PM A WEAK EF0 TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN. THREE PEOPLE WITNESSED THE TORNADO. THE DAMAGE PATH WAS ABOUT 150 TO 200 YARDS LONG WITH SOME MINOR DAMAGE REPORTED.
LOCATION 1/2 MILE EAST OF RED BLUFF AIRPORT
